section.heading: United States African-American Burial Grounds Preservation Program

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: The Secretary shall establish within the Service, in accordance with this chapter, a program to be known as the “United States African-American Burial Grounds Preservation Program”.

subsection.heading: Duties of Secretary.—
subsection.content: In carrying out the Program, the Secretary, in consultation with the National Trust for Historic Preservation and members of the African-American heritage community, shall develop a program for the provision of grants in accordance with section 308603(a).

subsection.heading: Donations.—
subsection.content: The Secretary may accept monetary donations to further the purposes of this chapter.

subsection.heading: Consent of Private Property Owner Required.—
subsection.chapeau: Burial grounds shall only be considered for a grant under the Program—

paragraph.content: with the consent of the property owner; and
at the request of an individual, landowner, private or nonprofit organization, State, Tribal, or local government, or other entity.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 117–328, div. DD, title VI, § 643(a), Dec. 29, 2022, 136 Stat. 5613.)
